record.title: Silver Nanoparticles Enhance Antimicrobial Efficacy of

evidence.evidence_text: The SEM images showed that the untreated control cells appeared intact, plump, and typically rod- shaped, with a smooth exterior (Figure 5a). In contrast, the bacterial cells exposed to anti- biotics alone or in combination with AgNPs at the MIC and FIC levels exhibited loss of membrane integrity, especially in the case of treatment with beta-lactam antibiotics (CAZ, IMI, MER) at the MIC level.

record.title: Silver Nanoparticles Enhance Antimicrobial Efficacy of

evidence.evidence_text: In contrast, the bacterial cells exposed to anti- biotics alone or in combination with AgNPs at the MIC and FIC levels exhibited loss of membrane integrity, especially in the case of treatment with beta-lactam antibiotics (CAZ, IMI, MER) at the MIC level. The cell walls became loose and porous, distorted from their normal shape, or even ruptured (Figure 5b–j).

record.title: Silver Nanoparticles Enhance Antimicrobial Efficacy of

evidence.evidence_text: We further observed the morphology of the bacteria treated with combinations of the antibiotics with AgNPs at the FBC (Figure 5k–n). The results clearly show that the bacte- rial cells were more severely damaged with this combination than with those at the MIC and FIC.

record.title: Silver Nanoparticles Enhance Antimicrobial Efficacy of

evidence.evidence_text: The results clearly show that the bacte- rial cells were more severely damaged with this combination than with those at the MIC and FIC. At the FBC, microscopic analysis of the bacterial cells revealed gross leakage and holes on the outer surface, with a bulgy, disfigured, and fragmented shape.

evidence.evidence_text: At the FBC, microscopic analysis of the bacterial cells revealed gross leakage and holes on the outer surface, with a bulgy, disfigured, and fragmented shape.
